note: legitimate exact match: abbrev TangentBundle := Bundle.TotalSpace E (TangentSpace I : M -> Type _), Mathlib/Geometry/Manifold/IsManifold/Basic.lean:1075, docstring 'The tangent bundle to a manifold, as a Sigma type', carries @[wikidata Q746550] in source

reason: object-level mismatch, not identity: Q186247 'vector field' (Wikidata description 'assignment of a vector to each point in a subset of Euclidean space') is a SECTION of the tangent bundle, not the tangent bundle itself -- Mathlib formalizes a vector field on a manifold as a section of TangentBundle (e.g. via Bundle.TotalSpace sections), a closely related but distinct object. No dedicated 'VectorField' decl exists in Mathlib to reassign to (checked: only Bundle/TangentBundle infrastructure, no standalone vector-field-on-manifold def). No field-of-study class on Q186247 (it's an object, not a discipline), so downgrade from exact/whatever to related rather than invocation.

note: legitimate closest-decl anchor: def mersenne (p : Nat) : Nat := 2^p - 1, Mathlib/NumberTheory/LucasLehmer.lean:38; Mathlib has no separate MersennePrime/IsMersennePrime predicate -- Mersenne primality is expressed inline as (mersenne p).Prime throughout LucasLehmer.lean (lucas_lehmer_sufficiency, lucas_lehmer_necessity, Nat.Prime.of_mersenne), and Archive/Examples/MersennePrimes.lean uses this same mersenne def for certified Mersenne-prime instances -- mersenne is the accepted anchor for this concept per existing catalog convention (cf. insphere-style container patterns), status should remain at whatever hedge is already applied given it's the numbers not the primality predicate, but no override needed here

reason: distinct-concept overclaim: Q3240929 'Largest known prime number' (Wikidata description 'largest prime number that is currently known') is a specific, time-varying record-holder fact (currently a Mersenne prime found via the Lucas-Lehmer test, but a distinct concept from the Mersenne numbers themselves) -- mersenne (2^p - 1) is the mechanism/vehicle used to find such primes, not a formalization of 'the largest known prime' as a concept (Mathlib has no decl tracking or asserting primality-record status; Archive/Examples/MersennePrimes.lean only certifies specific known Mersenne primes, doesn't formalize 'largest known'). Downgrade from whatever exact/high grading to related -- mersenne is the tool, not the record-holder concept. No field-of-study class on Q3240929.

Q842217 'Bayes estimator' (Wikidata description 'estimator or decision rule that minimizes the posterior expected value of a loss function') is precisely def IsBayesEstimator (l : Th -> Y -> ENNReal) (P : Kernel Th X) (k : Kernel X Y) (pi : Measure Th) : Prop, Mathlib/Probability/Decision/BayesEstimator.lean:103, docstring 'An estimator ... is said to be a Bayes estimator if it attains the Bayes risk for the estimation problem' -- this is a strictly better decl than bayesRisk (which formalizes the RISK VALUE, i.e. the minimum achieved, not the estimator itself). Verified IsBayesEstimator exists via grep in the same file that defines bayesRisk's dependency chain (Mathlib/Probability/Decision/BayesEstimator.lean imports Mathlib/Probability/Decision/Risk/Defs.lean where bayesRisk lives).

Q177571 'decision theory' (Wikidata class Q11862829, branch of applied probability theory) is the DISCIPLINE; Mathlib/Probability/Decision/ (BayesEstimator.lean, Risk/Defs.lean, Risk/Basic.lean covering avgRisk, bayesRisk, minimaxRisk, IsBayesEstimator, IsArgminEstimator) is a dedicated folder that is exactly the field-level home for decision theory in mathlib -- a much better container than pinning the discipline to the single bayesRisk decl (which itself is being reassigned away from Q842217 in this same batch).
